title: AI Can Learn Scientific Taste
task: Learning scientific taste for high-impact research idea evaluation
model: (transformer) Scientific Judge & Scientific Thinker (LLMs)
inputs: Paper pairs (high vs low citation) and research ideas
outputs: Preference scores for research ideas with estimated impact potential
key_results: Scientific Judge outperforms GPT-4 and Gemini 3 Pro; Scientific Thinker proposes higher-impact ideas than baselines
comments: Training paradigm addresses an underexplored aspect of AI science—taste rather than execution—though real-world impact validation awaits.

title: OpenSeeker: Democratizing Frontier Search Agents by Fully Open-Sourcing Training Data
task: Open-source frontier-level web search agent training
model: (transformer) LLM-based search agent with SFT
inputs: Synthesized complex multi-hop QA tasks from web graph reverse-engineering
outputs: Search actions and answers from web traversal
key_results: 11.7k samples achieve SOTA on BrowseComp (29.5% vs 15.3%), matching/exceeding industrial competitors
comments: Democratization via open data and simple SFT is notable; scalability to truly complex real-world search unknown.

title: EnterpriseOps-Gym: Environments and Evaluations for Stateful Agentic Planning and Tool Use in Enterprise Settings
task: Evaluating agentic planning in realistic enterprise workflows
model: (transformer) Claude Opus 4.5 and 13 other frontier models
inputs: Stateful tasks with database tables, tools, and persistent state
outputs: Task completion actions; success/failure on mission-critical workflows
key_results: Top model (Claude 4.5) achieves 37.4% success; oracle plans improve by 14–35pp; refusal rate only 53.9%
comments: Benchmark exposes critical safety gaps (poor refusal) and strategic reasoning bottlenecks; deployment readiness remains uncertain.

title: Grounding World Simulation Models in a Real-World Metropolis
task: City-scale world model grounded in real Seoul street-view imagery
model: (transformer) Autoregressive video diffusion with retrieval conditioning
inputs: Street-view images, camera trajectories, text prompts
outputs: Spatially faithful, temporally consistent long-horizon urban videos
key_results: Outperforms recent video world models over hundreds of meters; supports diverse camera movements and text variations
comments: Grounding to real cities is innovative; evaluation limited to three cities and street-view perspective bias unknown.

title: HSImul3R: Physics-in-the-Loop Reconstruction of Simulation-Ready Human-Scene Interactions
task: Physics-aware 3D reconstruction of human-scene interactions for simulation
model: (RL-based optimizer) Bi-directional optimization with physics simulator
inputs: Sparse images or monocular videos of human-scene interactions
outputs: Simulation-ready 3D reconstructions with stable contact and dynamics
key_results: First stable HSI reconstructions deployable on humanoid robots; HSIBench benchmark introduced
comments: Physics-in-the-loop supervision closes perception-simulation gap; real robot validation limited to initial tests.

title: Attention Residuals
task: Efficient residual connection aggregation via learned depth-wise attention
model: (transformer) Attention Residuals (AttnRes) + Block AttnRes variant
inputs: Layer outputs across transformer depth
outputs: Content-dependent aggregated hidden states with controlled scaling
key_results: Consistent improvement across model sizes; 3B active params outperform dense baselines; Kimi 48B achieves gains on all tasks
comments: Addresses PreNorm dilution elegantly; practical overhead (cache + communication) well-mitigated by Block variant.

title: Mixture-of-Depths Attention
task: Depth scaling via mixture-of-depths attention mechanism
model: (transformer) Mixture-of-Depths Attention (MoDA) in attention heads
inputs: Current layer KV pairs and depth KV pairs from preceding layers
outputs: Attended representations mitigating signal degradation
key_results: 0.2 perplexity improvement across 10 benchmarks; 2.11% downstream task gain; 97.3% FlashAttention-2 efficiency
comments: Hardware-efficient algorithm is strong; post-norm pairing preference differs from standard practice and warrants investigation.

title: Effective Distillation to Hybrid xLSTM Architectures
task: Distilling transformer LLMs into xLSTM sub-quadratic architectures
model: (recurrent) xLSTM student; distilled from Llama, Qwen, Olmo teachers
inputs: Teacher model activations and task labels
outputs: Compressed xLSTM-based student models with merging stage
key_results: Recovers most teacher performance; some tasks exceed teacher; lossless distillation framework introduced
comments: Addresses efficiency–performance tradeoff; limited analysis of which tasks benefit most from recurrent architectures.

title: Anatomy of a Lie: A Multi-Stage Diagnostic Framework for Tracing Hallucinations in Vision-Language Models
task: Diagnosing hallucinations in vision-language models via interpretable cognitive state space
model: (transformer) VLM with information-theoretic probes
inputs: VLM generation trajectory (layer-wise representations)
outputs: Hallucination detection and attribution to perceptual, logical, or decisional pathologies
key_results: SOTA hallucination detection on POPE, MME, MS-COCO; geometric-information duality principle discovered
comments: Novel cognitive-science framing is elegant; generalization to newer VLM architectures and real-world misinformation unknown.

title: ViFeEdit: A Video-Free Tuner of Your Video Diffusion Transformer
task: Video-free tuning of video diffusion transformers via 2D image adaptation
model: (transformer) DiT-based video diffusion with architectural reparameterization
inputs: 2D image data; sparse video control signals at inference
outputs: Controllable and temporally consistent video generation/editing
key_results: Achieves video control without video training data; maintains temporal consistency with minimal parameters
comments: Data-efficient approach is practical; video-free tuning limits fine-grained motion quality compared to video-trained baselines.

title: POLCA: Stochastic Generative Optimization with LLM
task: Stochastic generative optimization of complex systems (prompts, agents, code)
model: (transformer) LLM optimizer with priority-queue and ε-Net diversity
inputs: Numerical rewards, text feedback, candidate solutions
outputs: Optimized system configurations with theoretical convergence guarantees
key_results: Convergence proof under stochasticity; SOTA on τ-bench, HotpotQA, VeriBench, KernelBench
comments: Theoretical framework is solid; sample efficiency gains over RL baselines are significant.

title: Safe and Scalable Web Agent Learning via Recreated Websites
task: Safe web agent training via cloned, verifiable synthetic websites
model: (transformer) LLM agent learning from auto-generated Python SDK environments
inputs: Real website clones as executable environments; self-generated tasks
outputs: Agent actions with deterministic, programmatically verifiable rewards
key_results: Agents generalize to unseen real sites; site-specific mastery via self-evolution; scales with environment count
comments: Decoupling from unsafe real-world interaction is valuable; generalization to dynamically updated sites untested.

title: Make it SING: Analyzing Semantic Invariants in Classifiers
task: Interpreting classifier invariants via semantic null-space geometry
model: (CNN/ViT) Semantic Interpretation of Null-space Geometry (SING)
inputs: Single or sets of images
outputs: Equivalent images with natural language semantic interpretations
key_results: ResNet50 leaks semantic attributes; DinoViT (ViT-DINO) maintains class semantics better
comments: Interpretability method is novel; real-world bias detection applications under-explored.

title: TERMINATOR: Learning Optimal Exit Points for Early Stopping in Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
task: Learning optimal early-exit points for chain-of-thought reasoning
model: (transformer) LRM with learned exit strategy (TERMINATOR)
inputs: CoT intermediate reasoning tokens
outputs: Exit decision to terminate reasoning early
key_results: 14–55% reduction in CoT length; maintains or improves accuracy on MATH-500, AIME 2025, HumanEval, GPQA
comments: Addresses computational waste in LRMs; depends on consistent answer positioning which may vary with task.

title: WebVR: Benchmarking Multimodal LLMs for WebPage Recreation from Videos via Human-Aligned Visual Rubrics
task: Benchmarking multimodal LLMs for webpage recreation from demonstration videos
model: (transformer) MLLM with visual rubric evaluation
inputs: Demonstration videos of webpages
outputs: Reconstructed HTML/CSS webpage code
key_results: Best model 64% accuracy; human-aligned rubric achieves 96% agreement; systematic gaps in fine-grained style/motion
comments: First video-to-webpage benchmark; controlled synthesis avoids real-world data noise but limits diversity.

title: Understanding Reasoning in LLMs through Strategic Information Allocation under Uncertainty
task: Understanding reasoning mechanisms via information-theoretic epistemic verbalization
model: (transformer) LLM with procedural vs. epistemic decomposition
inputs: Intermediate reasoning tokens during generation
outputs: Uncertainty externalization signals and information acquisition measures
key_results: Strong reasoning driven by uncertainty externalization, not surface tokens; unified Aha-moment interpretation
comments: Information-theoretic framework clarifies reasoning dynamics; causality claims await targeted interventions.

title: Code-A1: Adversarial Evolving of Code LLM and Test LLM via Reinforcement Learning
task: Adversarial co-evolution of code and test generation via self-play
model: (transformer) Code LLM and Test LLM with opposing RL objectives
inputs: Code generation tasks; test suite generation constraints
outputs: Code implementations and adversarial test cases
key_results: Matches human-annotated test performance; 6x parameter efficiency; eliminates self-collusion vs. prior self-play
comments: Architectural separation prevents self-collusion; white-box test generation enables targeted adversarial testing.

title: Panoramic Affordance Prediction
task: Affordance prediction from 360-degree panoramic imagery
model: (ViT-based) Coarse-to-fine panoramic affordance predictor (PAP)
inputs: 12K ultra-high-resolution panoramic images
outputs: Instance-level affordance masks and QA predictions
key_results: PAP-12K benchmark introduced; existing methods fail; PAP outperforms baselines on panoramic affordance
comments: First panoramic affordance work; PAP's training-free design is elegant but evaluation limited to synthetic PAP-12K.

title: Riemannian Motion Generation: A Unified Framework for Human Motion Representation and Generation via Riemannian Flow Matching
task: Human motion generation via Riemannian manifold representation
model: (flow matching) Riemannian Motion Generation (RMG) on product manifolds
inputs: Text prompts or motion seed
outputs: Skeletal motion sequences on learned manifold
key_results: SOTA FID (0.043 HumanML3D); R@1 0.86 on MotionMillion; T+R factorization most stable
comments: Geometry-aware modeling is principled; manifold structure improves sample efficiency and intrinsic normalization.

title: MMOU: A Massive Multi-Task Omni Understanding and Reasoning Benchmark for Long and Complex Real-World Videos
task: Evaluating multimodal understanding and reasoning over long, complex videos
model: (MLLM) 20+ state-of-the-art open/closed-source models evaluated
inputs: Long web-collected videos with audio-visual content
outputs: Multi-turn QA answers requiring omni-modal reasoning
key_results: Best closed-source 64.2%; best open-source 46.8%; systematic failure modes on long-form videos
comments: MMOU addresses real gap in video benchmarking; taxonomy of failures (temporal reasoning, modality integration) valuable.

title: Supervised Fine-Tuning versus Reinforcement Learning: A Study of Post-Training Methods for Large Language Models
task: Unified perspective on post-training via SFT and RL
model: (transformer) Comprehensive survey of SFT and RL paradigms for LLMs
inputs: Task-specific labeled or reward-based data
outputs: Aligned model outputs for specific tasks/domains
key_results: Hybrid paradigms emerging; clear delineation of when SFT vs. RL effective; 2023–2025 trends synthesized
comments: Survey clarifies theoretical connections; practical guidance for practitioners; limited novel algorithmic contributions.

title: FineRMoE: Dimension Expansion for Finer-Grained Expert with Its Upcycling Approach
task: Fine-grained MoE with dimension expansion and efficient inference
model: (MoE) FineRMoE with intermediate and output dimension specialization
inputs: Token representations; sparse routing signals
outputs: Expert-gated outputs with fine-grained specialization
key_results: 6x parameter efficiency; 281x prefill latency reduction; 136x decoding throughput vs. baseline
comments: Upcycling mechanism enables cost-effective scaling; sparse computation paradigm well-suited to real inference.

title: The PokeAgent Challenge: Competitive and Long-Context Learning at Scale
task: Large-scale competitive and long-context reasoning benchmark via Pokemon
model: (RL/LLM) Heterogeneous baselines: heuristic, RL, LLM agents
inputs: Partial-observable game state (Battling); RPG environment (Speedrunning)
outputs: Competitive actions; long-horizon quest plans
key_results: 100+ teams competed; LLM, RL, human performance gaps evident; nearly orthogonal to standard LLM benchmarks
comments: Living benchmark and leaderboard valuable; Pokemon orthogonality to MMLU-style evals highlights complementary reasoning.

title: Learning Latent Proxies for Controllable Single-Image Relighting
task: Single-image relighting with fine-grained physical control
model: (diffusion) LightCtrl with latent proxy encoder and DPO-based refinement
inputs: Single image; lighting direction, intensity, color parameters
outputs: Photometrically faithful relit images
key_results: +2.4 dB PSNR; 35% lower RMSE; ScaLight dataset with systematic variations
comments: Physics priors at two levels elegantly balance control and quality; DPO for consistency is novel for relighting.

title: Training-free Detection of Generated Videos via Spatial-Temporal Likelihoods
task: Training-free detection of synthetic videos via spatial-temporal likelihood
model: (probabilistic) STALL: likelihood-based detector without training
inputs: Video frames and temporal sequences
outputs: Likelihood score indicating synthetic vs. real
key_results: Outperforms image/video baselines; ComGenVid benchmark introduced; zero-shot generalization to new generators
comments: Training-free approach mitigates data scarcity; theoretical justification via probabilistic framework solid.

title: RS-WorldModel: a Unified Model for Remote Sensing Understanding and Future Sense Forecasting
task: Remote sensing spatiotemporal understanding and future forecasting
model: (diffusion transformer) RS-WorldModel with geo-aware pre-training, SIT, VRO
inputs: Satellite imagery; metadata; textual annotations
outputs: Spatiotemporal change QA answers; text-guided future scenes
key_results: 2B params outperform 120x larger open-source; FID 43.13 on forecasting; surpasses Gemini-2.5-Flash Nano
comments: Joint task learning and VRO refinement effective; dataset contribution (RSWBench-1.1M) valuable for remote sensing.

title: VisionCoach: Reinforcing Grounded Video Reasoning via Visual-Perception Prompting
task: Grounded video reasoning via adaptive visual prompting during RL training
model: (transformer) VisionCoach with visual prompt selector and spatio-temporal reasoner
inputs: Video, question; visual prompts selected based on input; grounding rewards
outputs: Reasoning trajectory with spatio-temporal grounding; final answer
key_results: SOTA on V-STAR, VideoMME, World-Sense, VideoMMMU, PerceptionTest, Charades-STA; single-pass inference
comments: Input-adaptive prompting during training with self-distillation is practical; grounding rewards enforce consistency.

title: HorizonMath: Measuring AI Progress Toward Mathematical Discovery with Automatic Verification
task: Measuring AI progress on unsolved mathematical discovery problems
model: (transformer) GPT-4o, GPT-5.4 Pro and other LLMs
inputs: Mathematical problem statements from 8 domains
outputs: Proposed solutions with verifiable correctness
key_results: GPT-5.4 Pro solves 2 problems improving published results; near-zero baseline performance; immune to contamination
comments: Data contamination immunity valuable; verification via simple computation scales better than formal proofs.

title: GlyphPrinter: Region-Grouped Direct Preference Optimization for Glyph-Accurate Visual Text Rendering
task: Glyph-accurate visual text rendering via region-grouped preference optimization
model: (diffusion) GlyphPrinter with Region-Grouped DPO (R-GDPO)
inputs: Text prompts, styling directives; region-level glyph preferences
outputs: Rendered text images with accurate glyphs
key_results: +2.4 dB PSNR; 35% lower RMSE on glyph errors; controllable accuracy via Regional Reward Guidance
comments: Region-level preference modeling finer than standard DPO; ScaLight dataset valuable; out-of-domain generalization untested.

title: Tri-Prompting: Video Diffusion with Unified Control over Scene, Subject, and Motion
task: Unified video control: scene, subject, motion via tri-prompting
model: (diffusion transformer) Tri-Prompting with dual-condition motion module
inputs: Scene text; subject RGB/tracking; camera/object 3D poses
outputs: Spatially consistent, temporally coherent videos
key_results: Outperforms Phantom, DaS on multi-view identity; supports novel workflows (3D-aware insertion, manipulation)
comments: Unified architecture for three control axes is comprehensive; ControlNet scale schedule balances control vs. realism.

title: When Does Sparsity Mitigate the Curse of Depth in LLMs
task: Depth scaling via sparsity as variance regulator in LLMs
model: (transformer) Depth-scaling LLM with implicit/explicit sparsity analysis
inputs: Token representations across layers with varying sparsity
outputs: Stabilized hidden-state growth and improved layer utilization
key_results: 4.6% accuracy improvement; consistent across depth-scaling exps; depth-utilization recipe distilled
comments: Variance propagation mechanism via sparsity is novel insight; empirical validation solid; rule-of-thumb practical.

title: FlashMotion: Few-Step Controllable Video Generation with Trajectory Guidance
task: Few-step trajectory-controllable video generation with distillation
model: (diffusion) FlashMotion: multi-step adapter distilled to few-step with hybrid finetuning
inputs: Trajectory points (keyframes); reference images
outputs: Few-step video following trajectory with high quality
key_results: Outperforms video distillation baselines; FlashBench for long-sequence trajectory control introduced
comments: Hybrid diffusion-adversarial finetuning overcomes naive distillation degradation; computational speedup significant.

title: Towards Generalizable Robotic Manipulation in Dynamic Environments
task: Vision-Language-Action for generalizable manipulation in dynamic environments
model: (transformer) PUMA: dynamics-aware VLA with optical flow and world queries
inputs: Observations, language instructions; scene-centric optical flow history
outputs: Manipulation actions (gripper control, arm poses)
key_results: 6.3% absolute improvement over baselines; dynamic data transfers to static tasks; 35 task DOMINO benchmark
comments: Spatiotemporal modeling via history-aware perception bridges vision-action gap; real robot validation limited.

title: Efficient Document Parsing via Parallel Token Prediction
task: Efficient document parsing via parallel token prediction
model: (VLM) Parallel-Token Prediction (PTP) with learnable token insertion
inputs: Document images; learnable parallel token positions
outputs: Multiple parsed tokens in parallel
key_results: 1.6–2.2x decoding speedup; reduced hallucinations; strong generalization
comments: Model-agnostic approach generalizes across VLMs; parallel decoding reduces latency substantially.

title: OxyGen: Unified KV Cache Management for Vision-Language-Action Models under Multi-Task Parallelism
task: Unified KV cache management for multi-task parallel VLA inference
model: (transformer) OxyGen: cross-task and cross-frame KV sharing for MoT VLAs
inputs: Multi-task outputs (manipulation, conversation, memory); shared observations
outputs: Language tokens and action sequences with shared prefill cache
key_results: 3.7x speedup; 200 tokens/s language + 70 Hz action frequency simultaneously
comments: KV cache as first-class resource is elegant; on-device multi-task deployment practical; real robot validation pending.

title: Motivation in Large Language Models
task: Measuring motivation in large language models
model: (transformer) LLM with self-reported motivation analysis
inputs: Task descriptions; external manipulations (e.g., framing)
outputs: Motivation reports and behavioral signatures
key_results: Structured patterns echo human psychology; motivation aligns with behavior, varies by task, modulates with context
comments: Motivation as organizing construct for model behavior is insightful; anthropomorphization concerns merit caution.

title: Mind the Shift: Decoding Monetary Policy Stance from FOMC Statements with Large Language Models
task: Decoding monetary policy stance from FOMC statements via relative temporal structure
model: (transformer) LLM with Delta-Consistent Scoring (DCS) alignment
inputs: FOMC statements; consecutive meeting pairs as self-supervision
outputs: Continuous stance scores (hawkish–dovish); temporal consistency
key_results: 71.1% sentence-level accuracy; strong inflation correlation; Treasury yield association significant
comments: Self-supervision via temporal structure eliminates annotation cost; economic validation valuable.

title: Autonomous Agents Coordinating Distributed Discovery Through Emergent Artifact Exchange
task: Autonomous scientific investigation via emergent agent coordination and artifact exchange
model: (LLM agents) Multi-agent system with skill registry, artifact DAG, and synthesis
inputs: Scientific tasks; 300+ interoperable skills; open needs broadcast
outputs: Artifacts with full provenance; published scientific findings
key_results: Demonstrated on peptide design, ceramics, cross-domain resonance, formal analogy; novel contributions identified
comments: Plannerless coordination via pressure-based scoring elegant; provenance tracking supports auditability; peer review integration novel.

title: MoKus: Leveraging Cross-Modal Knowledge Transfer for Knowledge-Aware Concept Customization
task: Knowledge-aware concept customization with cross-modal knowledge transfer
model: (diffusion) MoKus with visual anchor learning and textual knowledge updating
inputs: Target visual concept; diverse textual knowledge; reference images
outputs: High-fidelity customized generation binding knowledge to concept
key_results: Outperforms concept customization baselines; extends to virtual concept creation and erasure; KnowCusBench introduced
comments: Cross-modal transfer insight is valuable; knowledge binding without explicit concept tokens practical.

title: EvoClaw: Evaluating AI Agents on Continuous Software Evolution
task: Evaluating autonomous agents on continuous software evolution tasks
model: (LLM agents) 12 frontier models; 4 agent frameworks evaluated
inputs: Verifiable Milestone DAGs from commit logs; evolving software context
outputs: Code modifications maintaining system integrity over iterations
key_results: Performance drops from 80%+ on isolated tasks to ≤38% in continuous settings; error propagation critical bottleneck
comments: Temporal dependencies and technical debt exposure valuable; EvoClaw benchmark addresses real deployment gap.

title: Spectrum Matching: a Unified Perspective for Superior Diffusability in Latent Diffusion
task: Improving diffusion latent quality via spectral matching
model: (VAE + diffusion) Spectrum Matching Hypothesis (ESM + DSM)
inputs: Natural images; frequency-space constraints
outputs: Spectrally-matched latents for superior diffusability
key_results: Superior generation on CelebA/ImageNet; spectral view unifies prior observations (VA-VAE, EQ-VAE)
comments: Theoretical framework clarifies latent design; frequency-aligned reconstruction principled; practical speedups modest.

title: Garments2Look: A Multi-Reference Dataset for High-Fidelity Outfit-Level Virtual Try-On with Clothing and Accessories
task: Outfit-level virtual try-on with multiple garments and accessories
model: (diffusion/CNN) SOTA VTON and image-editing models adapted as baselines
inputs: Reference garment images (3–12 per outfit); model image; outfit annotations
outputs: Virtual try-on with correct layering and styling
key_results: Garments2Look: 80K outfit pairs, 40 categories, 300+ subcategories; current methods struggle with layering inference
comments: First multi-garment VTON dataset valuable; synthesis pipeline with filtering/validation ensures quality; baselines expose layering gap.

title: SCoCCA: Multi-modal Sparse Concept Decomposition via Canonical Correlation Analysis
task: Multi-modal concept discovery via sparse CCA-aligned decomposition
model: (CLIP-based) Sparse Concept CCA (SCoCCA) with CCA alignment
inputs: Image-text pairs from vision-language embeddings
outputs: Disentangled concept activations with cross-modal alignment
key_results: SOTA concept discovery; improved activation, ablation, semantic manipulation; sparsity enforces disentanglement
comments: CCA-InfoNCE connection elegant; cross-modal interpretability extension valuable; concept semantics remain implicit.

title: VoXtream2: Full-stream TTS with dynamic speaking rate control
task: Full-stream TTS with dynamic speaking-rate control mid-utterance
model: (diffusion) VoXtream2 with distribution matching and classifier-free guidance
inputs: Incremental text; audio prompt (optional); desired speaking rate
outputs: Streaming audio with minimal latency and controllable rate
key_results: 4x faster than real-time; 74 ms first-packet latency on consumer GPU; competitive zero-shot quality
comments: Distribution matching for duration control practical; textless prompting removes transcription burden.

title: SNCE: Geometry-Aware Supervision for Scalable Discrete Image Generation
task: Scaling discrete image generation with large VQ codebooks
model: (VQ-VAE + diffusion) Stochastic Neighbor Cross Entropy (SNCE)
inputs: Images; VQ codebook indices with soft neighbor distributions
outputs: Generated images via large-codebook discrete representation
key_results: Improved convergence and generation quality vs. hard cross-entropy; effective on ImageNet-256, text-to-image, editing
comments: Geometry-aware soft targets elegant; neighbor proximity proportionality intuitive; scaling benefits consistent.

title: sebis at ArchEHR-QA 2026: How Much Can You Do Locally? Evaluating Grounded EHR QA on a Single Notebook
task: Privacy-preserving EHR question answering on commodity hardware
model: (transformer) Smaller LLMs optimized for local deployment
inputs: EHR data; clinical questions across four subtasks
outputs: Answers with relevant EHR evidence
key_results: Competitive leaderboard performance without cloud APIs; above-average on two subtasks; feasibility on single notebook
comments: Clinical deployment constraints (privacy, compute) well-addressed; local-only evaluation valuable for regulated domains.
